regions.text: Newton, who was born the year that Galileo died (1642), took the new line of thought much farther. Like Galileo he became a great watcher and recorder.
He asked the question 'how,' more than 'why,' answering by measuring and recording and comparing and proving. He saw the universe as a great machine.

He was able to put into words great natural laws. comparing: finding how things are the same or different. law: statements backed by power. natural: not made by man. natural law: statement of causes and effects.
FUTURE: will compare/break. PRESENT: compare(s)/break(s). PAST: compared/broke, broken.
